name: El Zurquillo Loop
name_original: El Punto Mira — circular desde Peraleda del Zaucejo
description: Enjoy an easy 2.2-mile (3.5 km) hike on the El Zurquillo Loop, gaining 372 feet (113 metres) with a viewpoint along the way.
region_intro: Hiking around Azuaga offers a variety of trails through diverse landscapes. The region is characterized by rolling hills, historical sites like ancient castles and Roman cities, and natural areas such as the Sierra de San Miguel and Sierra de Fuente del Arco. These features provide varied terrain for outdoor activities, from gentle walks to more challenging ascents. The trails often lead through open countryside, offering views of the surrounding Extremaduran landscape. Best hiking trails around Azuaga The most popular hiking route is Sierra de San Miguel Trail, a 5.9 miles (9.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 45 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the natural terrain of the Sierra de San Miguel. Another top favourite among local hikers is Sierra de Fuente del Arco Traverse Trail, a moderate 9.6 miles (15.4 km) path. This trail traverses the Sierra de Fuente del Arco, offering expansive views and varied topography. Local hikers also love the Alcazaba de Reina and Regina Turdulorum Trail, a 3.0 miles (4.8 km) trail leading through historical sites, often completed in about 1 hour 32 minutes. Hiking around Azuaga is defined by rolling hills, historical landmarks, and open countryside, with options for easy hikes around Azuaga as well as more demanding routes. The network offers options for different ability levels, including family friendly walks Azuaga. The routes in Azuaga are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 100 hikers have used komoot to explore Azuaga's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Azuaga?
A: There are over 30 hiking trails around Azuaga, offering a diverse range of experiences. These include 12 easy routes and 21 moderate trails, ensuring options for various fitness levels.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly hikes in Azuaga?
A: Yes, Azuaga offers several easy hikes suitable for beginners. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1199561145/miramontes-castle-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miramontes Castle Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a gentle 2.3-mile (3.7 km) trail that takes just over an hour to complete, offering pleasant views without significant elevation gain.

Q: What are some good circular walks in the Azuaga area?
A: Many trails around Azuaga are designed as circular routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1199599630/el-zurquillo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Zurquillo Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a great option, covering 2.2 miles (3.5 km) in about 1 hour 30 minutes. Another popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31850143" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Tendero loop from Fuente del Arco&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 3.6-mile (5.7 km) route.

Q: Are there family-friendly walking routes in Azuaga?
A: Yes, Azuaga has several routes suitable for families. Trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991585002/alcazaba-de-reina-and-regina-turdulorum-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcazaba de Reina and Regina Turdulorum Trail&lt;/a&gt; are relatively short and feature historical sites that can engage younger hikers. This 3.0-mile (4.8 km) route is rated as easy.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I see while hiking around Azuaga?
A: The region is rich in history. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991585002/alcazaba-de-reina-and-regina-turdulorum-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcazaba de Reina and Regina Turdulorum Trail&lt;/a&gt; leads directly through ancient historical sites. You can also explore nearby attractions such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5332798" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Granja de Torrehermosa&lt;/a&gt; or the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5884150" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza de España&lt;/a&gt; in Azuaga itself.

Q: What is the best season for hiking in Azuaga?
A: The best seasons for hiking in Azuaga are typically spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these months, temperatures are milder, and the landscape is often lush. Summers can be very hot, making early morning or late afternoon hikes preferable, while winters are generally mild but can be damp.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Azuaga?
A: The trails in Azuaga are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 10 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, from rolling hills to historical sites, and the well-maintained paths that offer expansive views of the Extremaduran countryside.

Q: Are there any trails that offer significant viewpoints or scenic vistas?
A: Yes, many trails in Azuaga provide excellent viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991580069/sierra-de-fuente-del-arco-traverse-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Fuente del Arco Traverse Trail&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, offers expansive views across the varied topography of the Sierra de Fuente del Arco, making it a popular choice for scenic hiking.

Q: What is the typical duration for hikes around Azuaga?
A: Hike durations vary widely. Shorter, easy routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1199561145/miramontes-castle-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miramontes Castle Loop&lt;/a&gt; can be completed in just over an hour. Moderate trails, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1199528953/sierra-de-san-miguel-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de San Miguel Trail&lt;/a&gt;, typically take around 2 hours 45 minutes, while longer routes can extend to 4-5 hours or more.

Q: Is parking available at the trailheads in Azuaga?
A: While specific parking information for each trailhead may vary, Azuaga and its surrounding villages generally offer public parking options. It's advisable to check local maps or route descriptions for designated parking areas near your chosen starting point, especially for trails beginning in smaller settlements.

Q: Are the hiking trails around Azuaga suitable for dogs?
A: Many of the natural trails around Azuaga are suitable for dogs, provided they are kept under control, especially when passing through agricultural areas or near livestock. It's always recommended to bring enough water for your pet, particularly during warmer months, as water sources may be limited on some routes.
tips: Observatory-Viewpoint The Mira Point is a platform built of wood, from which we have beautiful views of Peraleda and its surroundings.
The central point of the town of Peraleda Del Zaucejo. In the square are the Town Hall building and the parish church of San Benito Abad.

faqs.question: Is the El Zurquillo Loop suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, this trail is rated as easy. While it involves some elevation gain and a high starting altitude of 3,400 meters, its relatively short distance makes it manageable for those new to hiking, provided they are acclimatized to the elevation.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to hike this loop?
faqs.answer: On average, hikers complete this 3.5 km loop in about 1 hour and 3 minutes. Your actual time may vary depending on your pace and how long you spend enjoying the sights.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail winds through the Andean landscape, offering "fantabulous vistas." You'll encounter varied terrain typical of mountain paths, with some ascents and descents. The starting altitude of 3,400 meters means the air is thinner, which can make physical exertion feel greater.

faqs.question: What historical sites or landmarks can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: This route is rich in history. You'll pass by significant ancient Inca sites such as the Templo de la Luna (Temple of the Moon), an ancient Inca temple dedicated to fertility, and the archaeological site of Inkilltambo. You can also enjoy views from El Punto Mira.

faqs.question: Where does the El Zurquillo Loop trail start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail is located on the northern flanks of the Andean city of Cusco, Peru. While specific parking details aren't provided, trails in this region often have designated areas near popular access points or can be reached via local transport from Cusco.

faqs.question: Is the El Zurquillo Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Information regarding dog-friendliness for this specific trail is not available. It's always best to check local regulations or signage at the trailhead regarding pets before you go.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The Andean region generally has a dry season from May to October, which is often considered the best time for hiking due to clearer skies and less rain. However, always check local weather conditions before your visit, especially given the high altitude.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to hike this loop?
faqs.answer: Specific permit or entrance fee requirements for the El Zurquillo Loop are not detailed. However, some archaeological sites in the Cusco region, like those along this trail, may require an entrance ticket. It's advisable to inquire locally or check official tourism websites before your visit.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, approximately 27% of this route follows sections of the official trail named El Zurquillo.

faqs.question: Is the El Zurquillo Loop family-friendly?
faqs.answer: Given its 'easy' difficulty rating and relatively short distance, the El Zurquillo Loop can be suitable for families. However, the high altitude of 3,400 meters should be considered, especially for younger children or those not accustomed to elevation.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a hike on this trail?
faqs.answer: Due to the high altitude and varying weather, it's recommended to bring layers of clothing, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), plenty of water, and sturdy hiking shoes. Snacks are also a good idea for energy, and don't forget your camera for the "fantabulous vistas"!
